rubric.evidence: dense-composite-off-main-thread=pass (source: transferToImageBitmap). MEASURED at 2048×1536 (PerformanceObserver('longtask') + MessageChannel stall probe, headless Chrome): BEFORE the colourise (renderDepthColor: per-pixel sampleMap + putImageData) ran ~55ms on the main thread as ONE 55ms long task; AFTER the worker colourises via OffscreenCanvas.transferToImageBitmap and the page only draws the bitmap = ~2.3ms with ZERO long tasks. Colour-map changes now recolour off-thread too (no re-inference). Composite pixel-equality: measure-depth harness reports pixelDiffCount=0. verify: 0 console errors / 0 net failures / 0px overflow on top+basics+multi-model, light+dark.
rubric.notes: Composite main-thread cost 55ms → 2.3ms; crosses the 8ms frame slice before, well under it after. inp-stays-low (interaction) still manual.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / move heavy synchronous loops off-thread so they don't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference AND the dense colourise composite run off the main thread in a Web Worker; the depth map is colourised via OffscreenCanvas and transferred back as an ImageBitmap so the page runs no per-pixel loop (colour-map switches recolour off-thread too).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread + dense-composite-off-main-thread = pass; measure-depth: one 55ms main-thread long task before → 0 long tasks + 2.3ms drawImage after (2048×1536).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Measure INP / long tasks with real APIs (Long Animation Frames / Event Timing); don't infer responsiveness from 'uses a worker'.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — measured before/after with PerformanceObserver('longtask') + a MessageChannel main-thread stall probe on a 2048×1536 depth map, capturing the colourise's real main-thread ms.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: scratchpad/measure.mjs output retained; before 55ms long task, after none; pixelDiffCount=0.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout; avoid fixed widths that overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: verify — responsive-desktop/mobile conformance assertions checked (see rubric).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use intrinsic flex/grid (auto-fit minmax, flex-wrap, inline-size:100%) so control panels and canvas grids reflow without horizontal overflow at mobile and desktop widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .canvas-grid/.ba-wrap/.model-grid use auto-fit minmax + flex-wrap; controls use logical inline-size; responsive-desktop/mobile assertions pass (no overflow).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check: desktop+mobile automated-clean (0px overflow, 0 clipped controls).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/progress/ready/error via a labelled live region (role=status aria-live) and give every control an accessible name + visible focus.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared createModelLoader status is role=status aria-live=polite; page run/error status likewise; buttons-have-names + images-have-alt assertions pass.

openQuestions: Does depth-anything-v2-large produce semantically correct depth-estimation output on a real device (manual-evidenced)?
Are all controls keyboard-operable with visible focus on both classes?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for depth-anything-v2-large: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm real output + tap targets + focus + dialogs, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence) and enrich this critique.

Two remaining full-res per-pixel composites still run on the main thread on ladder pages: the practical depth-of-field blend (recomputes on every focus/aperture slider input) and the wild parallax warp (per animation frame). Both are outside this colourise retrofit; move them off-thread (worker warp/DoF with transferred RGBA/ImageBitmap, debounced) in a follow-up to close INP fully.

summary: Revision-1 seed for depth-anything-v2-large (depth-estimation, webgpu) derived from the real conformance run + responsive screenshots. Auto-checkable assertions are recorded with their true pass/fail/blocked state; manual-evidenced dimensions await an agent verdict. Not a final review.
